$comment_1: Signed reason-code dictionary for post-2026-07-21 Reg B compliance.
$comment_2: This JSON file is the AUTHORITATIVE mapping from loan features to adverse-action codes to Reg B categories. Bank counsel signs THIS FILE, not the LLM output. Refs: CFPB Circular 2022-03, CFPB final rule effective 2026-07-21, Freddie Mac Bulletin 2025-16 effective 2026-03-03, Decomposing Discrimination arxiv:2603.27510.
authority: Loredana C. Levitchi, primary-author contributor to Shadow (2026-06-19)

mappings.borrower_readable: Credit score below the eligibility floor required for standard approval. This includes both new borrower scores and any recent updates to the primary credit reference.
Ratio of monthly debt payments to monthly income exceeds the standard threshold. This reflects repayment capacity relative to existing obligations.
Loan amount is high relative to the appraised value of the collateral. Standard threshold prevents high-leverage exposure in adverse market conditions.
Portfolio-level risk metric (95% Value-at-Risk over 10-day horizon) exceeds the institution's risk appetite. This is an institutional-portfolio consideration, not a borrower-specific credit judgment.
The application requires review by a fair-lending officer prior to any final decision. This is a procedural gate, not a merit-based denial.
mappings.source_document: BRD Governance Controls (ECOA / Reg B integration)

mappings.borrower_readable: An anti-money-laundering, sanctions, or customer-identification compliance concern requires additional review. This includes screening under the Bank Secrecy Act, OFAC sanctions, USA PATRIOT Act §326 Customer Identification Program, and FinCEN Customer Due Diligence requirements. This is a regulatory eligibility concern, not a merit-based denial.
mappings.source_document: BSA / USA PATRIOT / FinCEN CDD / OFAC (see lib/aml-kyc-voice.js AML_FLAG_POLICY + KYC_STATUS_POLICY for the specific rule cited per finding)

signature.signer_name: PLACEHOLDER - to be signed by bank counsel
signature.signer_role: PLACEHOLDER - e.g. General Counsel, Chief Compliance Officer
